---
title: "plot_inflation_adjusted"
author: "Abidur Rahman"
date: "2026-04-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.



### plot_inflation_adjusted()

**Purpose:**  
Compares nominal and inflation-adjusted values on the same plot.

**What it does:**  
This function creates a base R line chart showing how values look before and after inflation adjustment. It makes the difference between nominal and real value easy to see.

**Use case:**  
Use this when showing how future money loses purchasing power over time.

**Inputs:**  
- `nominal`: A numeric vector of nominal values.
- `inflation_rate`: Annual inflation rate as a decimal.
- `years`: Time values matched to the nominal vector.
- `main`: Plot title.

**Output:**  
A base R comparison plot.

**Example:**  
`plot_inflation_adjusted(c(1000, 1500, 2000), 0.03, 0:2)`
